meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Portfolio Building to Fix Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master portfolio building to overcome debugging bottlenecks. An intermediate checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis kills freelance momentum. By pivoting to portfolio-focused development, you treat every bug as a modular component. Use Antigravity to architect scalable features and Claude Code to automate the resolution of technical debt. This shifts your workflow from reactive fixing to proactive, high-value asset creation.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal overflowing with stack traces, your client’s deadline looming like a storm front. The code, once elegant, has become a labyrinth of errors you can't navigate. This isn't just a bug; it's a productivity ceiling that threatens your reputation as a high-velocity freelancer.

But what if you stopped fighting the friction and started building the architecture? By shifting your mindset toward portfolio-grade modularity, you turn every debugging session into a reusable asset. With Antigravity and Claude Code, you don't just fix the error—you build a system that prevents it from ever returning.

steps.title: Architect the Module
steps.description: Isolate the buggy feature into a standalone unit. This makes it easier to debug and acts as a portfolio piece showing clean architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this feature into a modular, decoupled component. Ensure separation of concerns and provide clear interfaces for future extensibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor module into isolated service'

steps.title: Automate Trace Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse logs and trace the execution path, turning opaque errors into actionable insights.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the execution flow for this specific function. Identify the bottleneck causing the crash and suggest a non-breaking patch.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Patterns
steps.description: Add robust error handling that makes your code look professional and production-ready for client review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a decorator pattern for error handling across this module. Ensure all exceptions are caught and logged with contextual metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add robust logging and error boundaries'

steps.title: Generate Documentation Assets
steps.description: Automatically document your fix. This transforms a 'bug fix' into a 'feature improvement' for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical README section for this module, explaining the architectural decision and the fix implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate professional technical documentation for this feature'

steps.title: Validate via Test Suites
steps.description: Write automated tests to ensure the bug never recurs, adding another layer of professionalism to your delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for this module covering edge cases and successful execution paths.

pro_tips: Treat every bug fix as a Pull Request, even if you are the only dev; it forces you to write cleaner commit messages.
Use Claude Code to generate 'post-mortems' for your clients; it turns a delay into a demonstration of your technical rigor.
Keep a 'Component Library' folder in your repo; if a bug is hard to fix, it belongs in a library, not the main app loop.
Always run your production logs through Claude Code once a week to identify 'silent' errors before they break your app.
When you solve a recurring bug, publish the solution as a micro-blog post; it builds your authority and brings in better-paying clients.
content.next_steps: Once the module is stable, refactor your entire codebase using these patterns to create a 'gold-standard' template for all future client projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs define your freelance career. Build the systems that make you indispensable. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
